content.text:
{
  "module": "ui-design-system",
  "scores": {
    "visual_polish": 72,
    "domain_depth": 68,
    "data_richness": 65,
    "interaction_quality": 70,
    "ai_integration": 74,
    "mobile_first": 66,
    "performance": 71,
    "typescript_quality": 63,
    "error_handling": 67,
    "realtime": 73,
    "accessibility": 64,
    "security": 78,
    "differentiation": 69,
    "enterprise_readiness": 75
  },
  "overall_score": 70,
  "issues": [
    {
      "id": "ui-design-system-C2-001",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "error_handling",
      "title": "Empty state component never renders illustrations or CTAs — P0 unresolved from Cycle 1",
      "description": "src/components/ui/EmptyState.tsx exists but pages like RFIs.tsx, Submittals.tsx, PunchList.tsx still fall through to generic 'No data found' text. No illustration, no specific message, no primary action CTA. Violates design standards and the founder's explicit P0 requirement. Users see a blank table with no guidance on what to do next.",
      "prompt": "FILE: src/components/ui/EmptyState.tsx\n\nREWRITE the EmptyState component to accept: `icon: LucideIcon`, `title: string`, `description: string`, `action?: { label: string; onClick: () => void }`, `secondaryAction?: { label: string; onClick: () => void }`. Render a centered layout with: (1) the icon at 48px in a rounded bg-orange-50 circle, (2) title in 18px/600 weight, (3) description in 14px muted gray, (4) primary orange button if action provided, (5) secondary ghost button if secondaryAction provided. No generic fallback text allowed.\n\nTHEN update these files to pass specific props:\n- src/pages/RFIs.tsx: icon=FileQuestion, title='No RFIs yet', description='Create your first RFI to track questions and clarifications with the design team.', action={label:'Create RFI', onClick:()=>setShowCreate(true)}\n- src/pages/Submittals.tsx: icon=ClipboardList, title='No submittals yet', description='Track shop drawings, product data, and samples through the approval workflow.', action={label:'Create Submittal', onClick:()=>setShowCreate(true)}\n- src/pages/PunchList.tsx: icon=CheckSquare, title='Punch list is clear', description='No open punch items. Add items as you walk the site.', action={label:'Add Item', onClick:()=>setShowCreate(true)}\n\nACCEPTANCE: Each page shows illustration+icon, specific title, specific description, and a working CTA button when data array is empty. Zero instances of 'No data found' text remain in these pages."
    },
    {
      "id": "ui-design-system-C2-002",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "typescript_quality",
      "title": "Widespread `as any` casts in API client and endpoint files undermine type safety",
      "description": "src/api/client.ts uses `(target as any)[prop]`, `(target.from as (t: string) => any)`. src/api/endpoints/ai.ts uses `(data as any)?.created_at`. src/api/endpoints/editLocks.ts uses `(supabase as any).from(...)` repeatedly. These escape hatches mask real type errors and are especially dangerous in financial and auth code paths. Cycle 1 partial fix did not address these specific instances.",
      "prompt": "FILE: src/api/client.ts\nReplace the Proxy `get` trap's `(target as any)[prop]` with a properly typed accessor. Define `type SupabaseProxyTarget = typeof supabase` and use `Reflect.get(target, prop, receiver)` instead of `(target as any)[prop]`. Replace `(target.from as (t: string) => any)` with a typed helper that returns `ReturnType<typeof supabase.from>`.\n\nFILE: src/api/endpoints/editLocks.ts\nRemove all `(supabase as any).from(...)` calls. The `edit_locks` table must be added to `src/types/database.ts` under `Database['public']['Tables']` with columns: `entity_type: string`, `entity_id: string`, `locked_by_user_id: string`, `locked_at: string`, `expires_at: string`. Then replace all `(supabase as any)` with typed `supabase`.\n\nFILE: src/api/endpoints/ai.ts\nReplace `(data as any)?.created_at` with a typed variable: `const row = data as { created_at: string } | null` and access `row?.created_at`.\n\nACCEPTANCE: `tsc --noEmit --strict` produces zero errors related to these three files. No `as any` remains in client.ts, editLocks.ts, or ai.ts."
    },
    {
      "id": "ui-design-system-C2-003",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "visual_polish",
      "title": "Skeleton loaders not implemented for primary data tables — P0 unresolved from Cycle 1",
      "description": "src/components/ui/Skeletons.tsx exists but DataTable.tsx and VirtualDataTable.tsx do not render skeleton rows during loading state. Pages flash blank white or show spinners instead of pulse-animated skeleton rows matching table column widths. This violates the design standard requiring skeleton screens that match content shape exactly. Affects RFIs, Submittals, Budget, Change Orders pages.",
      "prompt": "FILE: src/components/ui/Skeletons.tsx\nAdd and export a `TableRowSkeleton` component that accepts `columns: number` and `rows?: number` (default 8). Each row is 48px tall with `animate-pulse` Tailwind class. Each cell renders a gray rounded rectangle (`bg-gray-200 rounded h-4`) at varying widths (alternate 60%, 40%, 80% per column index using modulo). Export a `TableSkeleton` that renders `rows` instances of `TableRowSkeleton`.\n\nFILE: src/components/shared/DataTable.tsx\nAdd a `loading?: boolean` prop. When `loading` is true, replace tbody content with `<TableSkeleton columns={columns.length} rows={8} />`. Do not show the actual rows until loading is false.\n\nFILE: src/components/shared/VirtualDataTable.tsx\nSame pattern: add `loading?: boolean`. When loading, render `<TableSkeleton columns={columns.length} rows={10} />` instead of the virtualized list.\n\nUpdate src/pages/RFIs.tsx, src/pages/Submittals.tsx, src/pages/ChangeOrders.tsx to pass `loading={isLoading}` to their DataTable instances.\n\nACCEPTANCE: During data fetch, table area shows pulse-animated skeleton rows. Zero blank white flashes. No spinner in table body. Skeleton column count matches actual column count."
    },
    {
      "id": "ui-design-system-C2-004",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "data_richness",
      "title": "Hardcoded placeholder values in project metrics endpoint not confirmed removed",
      "description": "Prior cycle flagged hardcoded values in src/api/endpoints/projects.ts (C1-003 marked partial). Metric cards on Dashboard and Portfolio pages may still display static numbers rather than computed values from financials and schedule data. If a CFO sees non-real numbers, trust in the platform is destroyed. The financial engine in src/lib/financialEngine.ts exists but wiring to project metrics is unconfirmed.",
      "prompt": "FILE: src/api/endpoints/projects.ts\nAudit every field returned by the project metrics functions (e.g., `getProjectMetrics`, `getPortfolioSummary`, or equivalent). Identify any field set to a hardcoded number literal (e.g., `budget_variance: 0`, `schedule_variance: 0`, `completion_percentage: 75`). Replace each with a real computation:\n- `budget_variance`: call `computeProjectFinancials()` from `src/lib/financialEngine.ts` and return `summary.variance`\n- `schedule_variance`: query `schedule_activities` where `project_id = projectId`, compute days behind as `planned_end - actual_end` on the last critical path item\n- `completion_percentage`: compute as `(sum of percent_complete * duration) / sum of duration` across all activities\n\nFILE: src/hooks/useProjectMetrics.ts\nEnsure the hook calls the real endpoint and does not override returned values with local defaults.\n\nACCEPTANCE: Dashboard metric cards show values that change when underlying data changes. No metric card displays a hardcoded integer. Add a comment `// COMPUTED: source = financialEngine` above each calculated field."
    },
    {
      "id": "ui-design-system-H2-005",
      "severity": "high",
      "dimension": "accessibility",
      "title": "DataTable and VirtualDataTable missing ARIA roles, live region, and keyboard navigation confirmation",
      "description": "C1-002 marked partial. src/components/shared/DataTable.tsx and VirtualDataTable.tsx lack explicit `role='grid'`, `role='row'`, `role='gridcell'` ARIA attributes. The `useTableKeyboardNavigation` hook was expanded but it is unclear if it is wired to both components. Screen reader users cannot navigate tables. WCAG 2.1 AA requires navigable data tables with proper semantics.",
      "prompt": "FILE: src/components/shared/DataTable.tsx\nAdd `role='grid'` to the `<table>` element. Add `role='row'` to each `<tr>`. Add `role='columnheader'` to `<th>` elements with `aria-sort='none'|'ascending'|'descending'` based on current sort state. Add `role='gridcell'` to `<td>` elements. Add `aria-rowcount={totalRows}` and `aria-colcount={columns.length}` to the table.\n\nWire `useTableKeyboardNavigation` from `src/hooks/useTableKeyboardNavigation.ts`: call the hook at the top of DataTable, pass the `tableRef`, and attach the returned `onKeyDown` to the table element. Ensure J/K navigate rows, Enter opens the row detail, Space selects.\n\nFILE: src/components/shared/VirtualDataTable.tsx\nApply the same ARIA role additions to the virtualized container. Add `aria-rowindex={startIndex + rowIndex + 1}` to each rendered row so screen readers announce position.\n\nFILE: src/components/ui/LiveRegion.tsx\nConfirm it renders a `role='status'` `aria-live='polite'` div. When sort or filter changes in DataTable, announce: 'Table sorted by [column] [direction]. [N] results shown.'\n\nACCEPTANCE: axe-core audit on DataTable produces zero violations. Keyboard-only navigation moves focus between rows with J/K and Enter opens detail. VoiceOver/NVDA announces column headers and sort state."
    },
    {
      "id": "ui-design-system-H2-006",
      "severity": "high",
      "dimension": "interaction_quality",
      "title": "CommandPalette Cmd+K wiring incomplete — global shortcut not confirmed active app-wide",
      "description": "C1-006 marked partial. src/components/shared/CommandPalette.tsx was expanded but src/App.tsx changes were only 18 lines. CommandPalette must be globally mounted once and respond to Cmd+K from any page. Without this, the Linear-style power-user workflow is broken. PMs and supers cannot navigate by keyboard.",
      "prompt": "FILE: src/App.tsx\nVerify that `<CommandPalette />` is rendered exactly once, outside all route `<Outlet>` components, at the top level of the app shell (alongside `<Sidebar>` and `<TopBar>`). It must not be inside any page component.\n\nFILE: src/components/shared/CommandPalette.tsx\nEnsure the component registers a global `keydown` listener via `useEffect` that calls `e.metaKey && e.key === 'k' && e.preventDefault() && open()`. The listener must be added on mount and removed on unmount. The palette must show:\n1. Recent pages (last 5 navigated)\n2. Quick actions: 'New RFI' (N then R), 'New Daily Log' (N then D), 'New Change Order' (N then C)\n3. Navigation items matching the sidebar structure\n4. A search input that filters all items with fuzzy matching\n\nFILE: src/hooks/useKeyboardShortcuts.ts\nAdd a `registerGlobal(key: string, handler: () => void)` function that stores handlers in a Map and deregisters them on unmount. CommandPalette should use this instead of raw addEventListener.\n\nACCEPTANCE: Pressing Cmd+K on any page (Dashboard, RFIs, Budget, Schedule) opens the palette within 50ms. Pressing Escape closes it. Typing 'rfi' filters to RFI-related actions. Enter on a result navigates or triggers the action."
    },
    {
      "id": "ui-design-system-H2-007",
      "severity": "high",
      "dimension": "mobile_first",
      "title": "Mobile touch targets below 44px minimum on key action buttons",
      "description": "C1-007 marked partial. src/components/mobile/MobileTaskCards.tsx and field capture buttons in src/components/field/QuickCapture.tsx have interactive elements below Apple's 44px minimum touch target guideline. On a dusty job site with gloves, this makes the app unusable for supers. The most critical mobile workflow fails the most basic field-first requirement.",
      "prompt": "FILE: src/components/mobile/MobileTaskCards.tsx\nAudit every interactive element (buttons, checkboxes, swipe action targets). Any element with height below 44px must be updated. Add `min-h-[44px] min-w-[44px]` Tailwind classes to all `<button>` elements. For icon-only buttons, wrap with a div that has `p-2` padding ensuring the total hit area is 44px. Add `touch-action: manipulation` CSS to prevent double-tap zoom delay.\n\nFILE: src/components/field/QuickCapture.tsx\nAll action buttons (photo capture, voice note, QR scan, crew check-in) must be at minimum 44px tall and 44px wide. The primary capture button should be 64px × 64px (thumb-friendly). Add `aria-label` to all icon-only buttons.\n\nFILE: src/components/layout/MobileLayout.tsx\nAdd a global CSS rule in the mobile layout scope: `button { min-height: 44px; }` to enforce the baseline. Add `touch-action: manipulation` to the layout root.\n\nACCEPTANCE: Automated axe-core mobile audit shows zero touch-target violations. Manual test: every tappable element on QuickCapture and MobileTaskCards is at least 44×44px in the browser device emulator at iPhone 14 Pro dimensions."
    },
    {
      "id": "ui-design-system-H2-008",
      "severity": "high",
      "dimension": "visual_polish",
      "title": "Design token system in tokens.css not enforced — Tailwind arbitrary values bypass the token layer",
      "description": "C1-008 marked partial. src/styles/tokens.css was added but components throughout the codebase continue to use Tailwind arbitrary values like `bg-[#F47820]`, `text-[#6B7280]`, `border-[#E5E7EB]` instead of the CSS custom properties defined in the token file. This means the token system provides no actual governance and one-off color values proliferate.",
      "prompt": "FILE: src/styles/tokens.css\nConfirm these CSS variables are defined: `--color-primary: #F47820`, `--color-success: #4EC896`, `--color-warning: #F5A623`, `--color-danger: #E74C3C`, `--color-info: #3B82F6`, `--color-bg-page: #F7F8FA`, `--color-bg-card: #FFFFFF`, `--color-sidebar: #0F1629`, `--color-text-primary: #1A1A2E`, `--color-text-muted: #6B7280`, `--color-border: #E5E7EB`.\n\nFILE: tailwind.config.js (or tailwind.config.ts)\nExtend the theme to map Tailwind color keys to the CSS variables: `colors: { primary: 'var(--color-primary)', success: 'var(--color-success)', warning: 'var(--color-warning)', danger: 'var(--color-danger)', 'bg-page': 'var(--color-bg-page)', 'text-primary': 'var(--color-text-primary)', 'text-muted': 'var(--color-text-muted)', border: 'var(--color-border)' }`. This enables `bg-primary`, `text-danger`, `border-border` classes.\n\nSearch and replace in src/components/Sidebar.tsx, src/components/TopBar.tsx, src/components/shared/DataTable.tsx: replace all `bg-[#F47820]` → `bg-primary`, `text-[#6B7280]` → `text-text-muted`, `border-[#E5E7EB]` → `border-border`.\n\nACCEPTANCE: Zero instances of `bg-[#F47820]`, `text-[#6B7280]`, or `border-[#E5E7EB]` in Sidebar.tsx, TopBar.tsx, DataTable.tsx. Token changes in tokens.css propagate visually without component edits."
    },
    {
      "id": "ui-design-system-M2-009",
      "severity": "medium",
      "dimension": "performance",
      "title": "VirtualDataTable migration to RFIs and Submittals pages unconfirmed complete",
      "description": "C1-009 marked partial. RFIs.tsx and Submittals.tsx were modified but it is not confirmed they use VirtualDataTable for large datasets. Projects with 500+ RFIs will render all rows to the DOM simultaneously, causing janky scrolling and high memory usage. Virtual scrolling is essential for the enterprise use case.",
      "prompt": "FILE: src/pages/RFIs.tsx\nLocate the table rendering block. If it uses `<DataTable>` or a raw `<table>` with `.map()`, replace with `<VirtualDataTable>` from `src/components/shared/VirtualDataTable.tsx`. Pass: `data={filteredRfis}`, `columns={rfiColumns}`, `rowHeight={48}`, `containerHeight={600}`, `onRowClick={(rfi) => navigate(rfi.id)}`, `loading={isLoading}`.\n\nFILE: src/pages/Submittals.tsx\nSame pattern: replace the table with `<VirtualDataTable>` using `data={filteredSubmittals}`, `columns={submittalColumns}`, `rowHeight={48}`, `containerHeight={600}`, `onRowClick={(sub) => navigate(sub.id)}`, `loading={isLoading}`.\n\nFILE: src/components/shared/VirtualDataTable.tsx\nConfirm the component uses `react-window` or manual offset calculation to only render visible rows plus an overscan of 5. If react-window is not installed, add it: `npm install react-window @types/react-window`. Use `FixedSizeList` with `itemSize={48}`.\n\nACCEPTANCE: Chrome DevTools Performance tab shows fewer than 100 DOM nodes in the table body when 500 RFI rows are loaded. Scrolling at 60fps with no dropped frames. Row click navigates to detail view."
    },
    {
      "id": "ui-design-system-M2-010",
      "severity": "medium",
      "dimension": "domain_depth",
      "title": "Ball-in-court indicator missing from RFI and Submittal list views",
      "description": "The module spec explicitly requires a ball-in-court indicator showing who owns the next action on every RFI and submittal row. This is a core construction workflow concept — without it, PMs cannot instantly see which items are waiting on them vs. the architect vs. the subcontractor. No evidence this column exists in current list views.",
      "prompt": "FILE: src/pages/RFIs.tsx\nAdd a `ball_in_court` column to the RFI table columns definition. The value comes from `rfi.ball_in_court` (string: 'GC', 'Architect', 'Engineer', 'Owner', 'Sub'). Render it as a colored badge: GC=blue, Architect=purple, Engineer=teal, Owner=orange, Sub=gray. Badge style: `px-2 py-0.5 rounded-full text-xs font-medium` with background at 10% opacity of the color and full-opacity text.\n\nFILE: src/pages/Submittals.tsx\nSame pattern. Add `ball_in_court` column with values: 'Sub', 'GC', 'Architect'. Use the same badge component.\n\nFILE: src/api/endpoints/rfis.ts\nConfirm `ball_in_court` is selected in the Supabase query (`select('*, ball_in_court, ...')`). If the column does not exist on the RFIs DB type, add it to `src/types/database.ts` as `ball_in_court: string | null`.\n\nFILE: src/api/endpoints/submittals.ts\nSame: confirm `ball_in_court` is included in the select query.\n\nACCEPTANCE: Every RFI row in the list view shows a colored ball-in-court badge. Every Submittal row shows a ball-in-court badge. Clicking the column header sorts by ball_in_court value. Badge colors match the construction-domain role color scheme."
    }
  ],
  "invented_features": [
    {
      "title": "Design Token Governance Dashboard",
      "description": "A developer-facing page at /admin/design-system that renders every token from tokens.css as a live swatch with its variable name, hex value, and a list of components using it. Clicking a token shows all Tailwind classes that reference it. A 'Token Health' score shows percentage of color usages that go through the token system vs. arbitrary values. This creates accountability for visual consistency and speeds up theming for white-label enterprise clients."
    },
    {
      "title": "Field-Mode UI Toggle",
      "description": "A persistent toggle in the top bar (or auto-detected via GPS/time-of-day) that switches the entire app into Field Mode: increases all font sizes by 2px, boosts touch targets to 56px, reduces nav depth to one level, removes all non-essential UI chrome, switches to high-contrast colors for sunlight readability, and enables always-on voice input. Supers activate it once at 6am and the app stays in field mode until they manually exit or leave the job site radius."
    },
    {
      "title": "Skeleton-to-Content Transition System",
      "description": "A shared animation library in src/lib/animations.ts that provides a `useContentReveal` hook. Components register their loading state with this hook. When data arrives, skeleton cells fade out and real content fades in with a 150ms staggered delay per row (0ms, 30ms, 60ms, etc.). This creates a polished waterfall reveal effect rather than a jarring instant swap, matching the quality bar of Linear and Stripe."
    }
  ],
  "competitive_intel": "Procore's mobile app remains a desktop adaptation — touch targets are inconsistent and there is no field mode. SiteSync's design system has the right foundations (token file, skeleton components, virtual tables, keyboard navigation) but Cycle 1 fixes were incomplete. The gap to close is execution: every P0 item from Cycle 1 is partially done but not shipped. Fieldwire beats SiteSync on mobile UX right now purely because their touch targets and offline states are battle-tested. Linear beats SiteSync on keyboard navigation because their command palette is globally registered from day one. The design system score of 70 means SiteSync is above Procore's 2010-era UX but not yet at the Linear/Stripe standard the founder demands. Completing the Cycle 1 partials — skeletons, empty states, token enforcement, keyboard shortcuts, touch targets — would push the score to ~84 and create a meaningful field-first advantage over Procore.",
  "summary": "The SiteSyncAI UI design system has strong architectural bones — a token file, skeleton components, virtual data table, keyboard shortcut hook, and command palette all exist in the codebase. However, Cycle 1 left 9 of 9 issues in partial or unresolved state, meaning the platform's visible quality still falls short of the Apple/Linear standard the founder demands. The most critical gaps are: (1) empty states still showing generic text instead of illustrated, actionable states on RFI, Submittal, and Punch List pages, (2) widespread `as any` casts in the API client and editLocks endpoint that undermine TypeScript safety in financial-critical code paths, (3) skeleton loaders not wired to DataTable/VirtualDataTable loading states causing blank white flashes, and (4) real metric computations not confirmed connected to dashboard cards, risking CFO trust. Mobile touch targets remain below Apple's 44px minimum on the most-used field workflows, and the Cmd+K command palette is not confirmed globally mounted. The design token system exists but is bypassed by Tailwind arbitrary values throughout Sidebar and TopBar. With focused execution on the 10 issues in this audit — all of which are targeted, small-scope fixes — the module can reach an 84+ score and deliver the premium construction OS experience that makes Procore look obsolete."
}
